title: 11L INT6 + Backward-Looking Per-Document LoRA TTT
author: haimianbaobao007
body:
## Summary

11-layer transformer with INT6 QAT, backward-looking per-document LoRA TTT evaluation.

### Architecture
- 11 layers, d=512, GQA 8/4, MLP 3x
- LeakyReLU(0.5)² activation (preserves negative gradient flow)
- BigramHash(4096,128) + SmearGate
- U-Net skip connections
- Tied embeddings

### Training
- Muon optimizer (matrices) + Adam (scalars)
- EMA decay 0.997
- INT6 QAT (uniform quantization)
- Auto warmdown (15% of compute budget)
- Artifact: ~11 MB (well within 16 MB)

### Evaluation: Per-Document LoRA TTT
- LoRA rank 8 on attention Q and V projections
- 10 epochs per document, cosine LR decay (0.01 → 0.0001)
- **Backward-looking (score-first)**: each 256-token chunk is scored BEFORE LoRA trains on it
- Per-document independent LoRA with reset between documents
- Last chunk not trained in final epoch (zero horizon benefit)
- Documents < 512 tokens evaluated without TTT

### Key Insight
LoRA constrains the adaptation subspace to rank 8, preventing the overfitting that plagues full-parameter TTT on quantized models. Per-document reset ensures no cross-contamination between documents.

### Validation (RTX 5090, 500 steps, 100 docs)
- Base INT6: 1.685 BPB
- After LoRA TTT: **1.189 BPB** (-29.5%)

### Logs
Will be provided after official H100 run.

### Score
Pending official H100 evaluation.
